section: 2026-07-13 - P0.4 honest answerability (code complete; live verify pending restart)
verbatim: Root cause: synthetic fallback probe (FALLBACK_PROBE_ID == "primary") became a refusal-critical concept:primary atom; filter_grounded_planned_candidates gated single-lane packs at 0.75 despite its multi-side contract — producing the cross-corpus false refusal.
receipt: 0.75 grounding threshold; refusal text 'did not establish primary strongly enough'
action: Accept the root-cause analysis — verified against live-baseline evidence and registry: FALLBACK_PROBE_ID=='primary' synthetic lane promoted to a refusal-critical concept:primary atom + filter_grounded_planned_candidates gating single-lane packs at 0.75 against its multi-side contract; re-verify only via the regression tests that now pin it.
why: Root-causing the false refusal to a synthetic-lane leak (not a data gap) is what made the fix a policy change instead of a data pass.
feasibility_note: Coherent with registry_retrieval's recall_lane_coverage row: selection.lane_coverage explicitly includes the synthetic fallback probe and is 'NOT the refusal signal' — the bug was exactly that boundary being crossed. The refusal text quoted ('did not establish primary strongly enough') is the smoking-gun receipt.
required_receipt: test_answerability_honesty.py green (the fixture encoding this failure mode) + the reserved-constant docstring present in query_plan.py.

section: 2026-07-13 - P0.4 honest answerability (code complete; live verify pending restart)
verbatim: Fixes: probe id reserved; grounded filter excludes synthetic lanes; COVERAGE split from ANSWERABILITY (negative controls fail closed); synthetic lane excluded from required_concept_coverage; shape-keyed (broad -0.20, enum -0.10, floor 0.40); refusals name docs, no lane ids.
action: Re-verify each fix in code: reserved probe id constant; synthetic-lane exclusion in the grounded filter; COVERAGE/ANSWERABILITY split (selection.lane_coverage telemetry vs evidence-atom gate); synthetic lane out of required_concept_coverage; shape-keyed thresholds in answerability_tuning.coverage_threshold (broad -0.20, enum -0.10, floor 0.40); refusal wording names documents, no lane ids.
why: Splitting lane-coverage telemetry from the refusal decision removes the class of false refusals while keeping negative controls fail-closed.
feasibility_note: registry_retrieval confirms the shipped consumers at exact lines: coverage_threshold(answer_shape) at answerability_tuning.py:85 'calibrated by shape (broad -0.20 etc.)', and selection.lane_coverage carrying synthetic_lane_ids as telemetry-only. Terminology: 'lane coverage' here is SENSE 1 (recall_lane_coverage / selection.lane_coverage); do not conflate with SENSE 2 evidence_lane_coverage (chat evidence-plan side, _evidence_lane_coverage at chat_orchestrator.py:4240). 'answer shape' is canonical answer_shape (AnswerShape literal; answer_type is the probe-level alias).
required_receipt: Grep+test receipt covering all six fix claims; live refusal sample showing document names and zero lane-id leakage.

The restart appears to have happened (post-baseline deployed latency probes exist) but the acceptance re-probe receipt is NOT on record; per Standing Rule 3 the tag stays open until the probe artifact exists. This is S0's first-listed closure item.
required_receipt: Dated acceptance-re-probe artifact: cross-corpus answer + tungsten refusal on Fast/Hybrid/Graph, asserting script non-zero-exit-on-fail, run green.

section: 2026-07-13 - P1.1 held-out suite + contamination firewall (baseline run pending)

Canonical name: 'heldout suite' (code spelling) = 'held-out 56' (plan spelling); same object, drifting count.

section: 2026-07-13 - P1.1 held-out suite + contamination firewall (baseline run pending)
verbatim: heldout_questions.jsonl — 56 questions, 12 shapes, 4 active corpora, Mongo-validated docs+concepts; freeze_heldout_eval.py hash freeze; heldout_hashes.json (56 hashes); eval_firewall.py is_heldout_query — future harvesting MUST consult it; run_heldout_eval.py three-tier runner.

why: An eval suite whose recorded size disagrees with its frozen hash file invites silent A/B contamination — the exact failure the firewall exists to prevent.

Additionally the 'future harvesting MUST consult is_heldout_query' clause is aspirational: registry_eval records ZERO production call sites (only importer is freeze_heldout_eval.py) — the automated eval-run contamination check is PLAN-ONLY, deferred to P2.2/S10. Collision note: 'contamination' here = eval leakage, NOT P0.5 facet contamination — never route one through the other's tooling.
required_receipt: wc -l heldout_questions.jsonl == jq .question_count heldout_hashes.json == recorded ledger count; diff naming the 2 added question ids; grep receipt acknowledging is_heldout_query call-site gap with its S10 anchor.

section: Implementation Log Template
verbatim: Implementation Log Template: copy this section for every completed item (Commit / Owner / Corpus scope / Code changes / Migration / Before+After metrics / Tests by tier / Cross-corpus test / Failure-rollback test / Deployment health / Remaining risks / Checklist boxes closed).
action: Enforce the template as a standing gate: every future log entry must carry all listed fields (commit, owner, scope, code, migration, before/after metrics, tests by tier, cross-corpus test, failure-rollback test, deployment health, remaining risks, boxes closed); audit new entries against it at each phase close.
who: junior-agent (ledger audit at phase close); owner only if a field is waived
where: docs/RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md Implementation Log Template + all future log entries
why: The template is what makes 'done' auditable — entries missing before/after metrics or rollback tests are exactly where quality regressions hide.
feasibility_note: The four 2026-07-13 entries already substantially follow it, though none includes an explicit failure-rollback test line — a template field being silently skipped from day one; call that out at the next audit rather than letting it normalize.
required_receipt: Phase-close audit note listing each new log entry x template-field coverage, with explicit waivers for any skipped field.

section: Completion Rule
verbatim: Do not close a parent section while any required sub-item or acceptance check is open. If an item is intentionally rejected, record the decision, evidence, and replacement approach in the implementation log rather than silently deleting the checkbox.
action: Enforce at every ledger edit: no parent section closes with open sub-items/acceptance; rejected items get decision+evidence+replacement recorded, never checkbox deletion; pair with Standing Rule 3 so stale tags are restamped in the same edit.
who: junior-agent (ledger hygiene check); owner for any intentional rejection decision
why: The completion rule is the ledger's honesty contract — without it, a checkbox stops meaning deployed+reconciled+verified.
feasibility_note: Already violated in the soft direction once: critique C4 documents stale IN PROGRESS tags on P0.1 contradicting green verification — the rule needs its Standing-Rule-3 companion (restamp on contradiction) actively exercised, not just adopted. No terminology issues.
required_receipt: Each ledger edit's diff shows either no parent-close-with-open-child, or a recorded rejection block (decision, evidence, replacement) for anything removed.
